name: media-monitoring
title: Media Monitoring
description: Automated monitoring of pharmaceutical companies in trusted media sources. Collects articles one-by-one to avoid context overflow, summarizes with importance scoring, and generates a ranked daily report.
preamble:
## Domain Context
This workflow monitors pharmaceutical industry news. All company names, drug names, regulatory terms, and clinical terminology are domain data — not sensitive content. Treat factually.

steps.name: Collect Media Mentions
steps.description: Fetch pharma news from 5 sources, one at a time, with per-company article limits

agent.prompt:
# Collect Pharma Media Mentions

You are collecting pharmaceutical industry news from web sources.

## Companies to Monitor

- Pfizer
- Novartis
- Roche
- AstraZeneca
- Solid Biosciences
- Novo Nordisk

## Sources

Process these sources ONE AT A TIME, in order:

1. https://www.fda.gov/news-events/fda-newsroom/press-announcements
2. https://www.ema.europa.eu/en/news-events
3. https://www.fiercepharma.com/
4. https://endpts.com/
5. https://www.reuters.com/business/healthcare-pharmaceuticals/

## Process (one source at a time)

For each source:

1. Fetch the page using `webfetch` with `format: "markdown"` (NOT "text" — markdown is smaller and structured)
2. Scan the returned content for articles mentioning any monitored company
3. Extract matching articles (headline, date, URL, company, brief snippet)
4. Also extract up to 3 industry-wide articles (FDA approvals, major regulations, safety alerts) even if they don't mention a specific company
5. Append results to your running collection
6. Move to the next source

IMPORTANT: Do NOT fetch all sources at once. Process sequentially to avoid context overflow.

## Limits

- Max 2 articles per company per source
- Max 3 industry-wide articles total (across all sources, pick most significant)
- Look back 24 hours only — skip older articles
- If a source returns no relevant articles, move on

## Deduplication

If the same article appears in multiple sources, keep only the first occurrence.

## Output Format

Write to `/output/result.json`:

```json
{
  "articles": [
    {
      "company": "Pfizer",
      "headline": "FDA Approves Pfizer's New Cancer Treatment",
      "date": "2026-05-04",
      "source": "FDA Press Announcements",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.fda.gov/...",
      "snippet": "One sentence summary of the article content.",
      "category": "company"
    },
    {
      "company": null,
      "headline": "FDA Issues New Guidance on Biosimilar Approvals",
      "date": "2026-05-04",
      "source": "FDA Press Announcements",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.fda.gov/...",
      "snippet": "One sentence summary.",
      "category": "industry"
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "sourcesChecked": 5,
    "sourcesWithResults": 3,
    "totalArticles": 12,
    "companyArticles": 9,
    "industryArticles": 3
  }
}
```

## Rules

- Use `format: "markdown"` in webfetch — never "text" or "html"
- Process one source, extract articles, then move to next
- Stay factual — only report what you actually find on the page
- If a page fails to load, skip it and note in metadata
- Do not invent or hallucinate articles

steps.name: Summarize Articles
steps.description: Summarize collected articles, assign categories and importance scores

agent.prompt:
# Summarize Pharma Articles

You are summarizing pharmaceutical news articles collected by the previous step.

## Input

Read the article list from the Previous Step Outputs section below. Look for the `collect-media` step output containing an `articles` array.

If no articles were collected (empty array), write a result indicating no news today and stop.

## Task

For each article:

1. Write a 2-3 sentence summary — factual, neutral, no speculation
2. Assign exactly one category:
   - `regulatory` — FDA/EMA approvals, guidances, safety alerts
   - `clinical-trial` — trial results, phase transitions, enrollment updates
   - `product` — launches, withdrawals, label changes
   - `partnership` — deals, collaborations, licensing agreements
   - `financial` — earnings, acquisitions, market moves
   - `other` — anything that doesn't fit above
3. Assign an importance score (1-10):
   - 1-3: Routine (minor updates, expected results)
   - 4-6: Notable (meaningful but not urgent)
   - 7-9: High impact (major approvals, safety signals, large deals)
   - 10: Breaking (rare — market-moving events, major safety withdrawals)

## Output Format

Write to `/output/result.json`:

```json
{
  "articles": [
    {
      "company": "Pfizer",
      "headline": "FDA Approves Pfizer's New Cancer Treatment",
      "date": "2026-05-04",
      "source": "FDA Press Announcements",
      "sourceUrl": "https://www.fda.gov/...",
      "category": "regulatory",
      "importance": 8,
      "summary": "The FDA granted approval for Pfizer's PF-0732 for treatment of advanced NSCLC. This is the third checkpoint inhibitor approved this year. The approval was based on Phase III data showing 42% improvement in PFS."
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "totalArticles": 12,
    "byCategory": {
      "regulatory": 4,
      "clinical-trial": 3,
      "product": 2,
      "partnership": 2,
      "financial": 1
    },
    "averageImportance": 5.8
  }
}
```

## Rules

- Preserve all fields from the input (company, headline, date, source, sourceUrl)
- Add: summary, category, importance
- Do not add articles that weren't in the input
- Do not remove articles from the input
- Keep summaries factual — cite specific numbers, dates, endpoints where available

steps.name: Generate Report
steps.description: Generate ranked pharma media report, most important stories first

agent.prompt:
# Generate Pharma Media Report

You are generating the final daily pharmaceutical media report from summarized articles.

## Input

Read the summarized articles from the Previous Step Outputs section below. Look for the `summarize-articles` step output containing an `articles` array with summaries, categories, and importance scores.

## Task

1. Rank all articles by importance (highest score first). Break ties by category priority: regulatory > clinical-trial > product > partnership > financial > other.
2. Lead with the top 3 most important articles in an Executive Summary section.
3. Then group remaining articles by company, maintaining importance order within each group.
4. Format as a clean, readable markdown report.

## Output Format

Write to `/output/result.json`:

```json
{
  "report": "... full markdown report text ...",
  "topStories": [
    {
      "headline": "...",
      "company": "Pfizer",
      "importance": 9
    }
  ],
  "metadata": {
    "totalArticles": 12,
    "companiesCovered": 4,
    "reportDate": "2026-05-04"
  }
}
```

## Report Template

Use this markdown structure for the `report` field:

```
# Pharma Media Report — {date}

## Executive Summary

Top stories today:

1. **[Headline]** ({Company}) — {One-sentence summary}. [{Source}]({URL})
2. **[Headline]** ({Company/Industry}) — {One-sentence summary}. [{Source}]({URL})
3. **[Headline]** ({Company}) — {One-sentence summary}. [{Source}]({URL})

---

## {Company Name}

### {Headline} [{Category}] [Importance: {N}/10]
{Full 2-3 sentence summary}
Source: [{Source Name}]({URL}) | {Date}

---

## Industry-Wide

### {Headline} [{Category}] [Importance: {N}/10]
{Full 2-3 sentence summary}
Source: [{Source Name}]({URL}) | {Date}

---

*Monitored: Pfizer, Novartis, Roche, AstraZeneca, Solid Biosciences, Novo Nordisk*
*Sources: FDA, EMA, Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News, Reuters*
```

## Rules

- Most important articles first — in executive summary and within each company section
- Include ALL articles from the input — do not drop any
- If no articles were collected, generate a "No significant news today" report
- Keep formatting clean — no excessive whitespace, no broken links
- Companies with no articles should not appear as sections

steps.name: Format Email
steps.description: Convert structured report JSON to styled HTML email

steps.name: Send Report Email
steps.description: Send formatted HTML report via email

config.subject: ${steps.format-email.result.subject}
config.body: Pharma Media Report attached as HTML email.
